name: Halibut Pecante
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 lb Halibut Fillet, about 1 inch thick
1/3 cup Roasted Pecans, coarsely crumbled by
2 tbsp Roast Garlic, peeled and pulped
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat butter in a frying pan until foaming subsides, then reduce heat to medium-low.
2. Fry fish five minutes per side, being careful when turning it not to break it apart.
3. While the fish is frying, combine sauce ingredients.
4. When the fish is done, remove it to plates or serving platter.
5. Deglaze the pan with the sauce, stirring briskly until it begins to thicken just slightly.
6. Pour sauce decorously over the fish and serve.
NOTES:
Other mild, tasty vinegars could be substituted. If all you have are wine or cider vinegars, use two tablespoons of that and two tablespoons of some kind of unsweetened fruit pulp or juice.
Since you don't have muscadine wine, substitute a dry Gewürztraminer or maybe a Riesling - anything dry with a spicy or fruity flavor.
Other thick, firm fish could be used. Fry it five minutes per side for each inch of thickness.
